text:
**Owner:** IT Operations
**Last Updated:** January 2025

---

Acme Corp provides employees with the tools they need to do their best work. This document covers how to request standard equipment, upgrade existing hardware, and what policies apply to remote workers.

All equipment requests are submitted via **ServiceNow** at service.acmecorp.com.

---

Every new hire receives the following upon joining:

- **Laptop:** 14" MacBook Pro (M3) or Dell XPS 15 (Windows) — based on role/team preference indicated in the onboarding form
- **External monitor:** 27" Dell UltraSharp 4K USB-C monitor
- **Peripherals:** Wireless keyboard, wireless mouse, USB-C hub
- **Headset:** Jabra Evolve2 55 (for customer-facing or frequent-meeting roles) or Poly Voyager (standard)
- **Power adapter:** Compatible with issued laptop

Engineers and designers may request upgraded specs (16" MacBook Pro M3 Max or equivalent Windows spec). This requires manager approval and is subject to budget availability.

---

| Category | Budget Cap |
|---|---|
| New hire equipment (full setup) | $2,500 |
| Hardware refresh (laptop only) | $1,500 |
| Peripheral replacement (single item) | $300 |
| Engineering/Design upgrade package | $3,500 |

Requests exceeding the standard budget cap require **VP-level approval** in addition to manager approval.

---

New hire equipment is automatically provisioned by IT based on the start date in Workday. No ticket is needed — your hiring manager should confirm your laptop preference (Mac or Windows) in the onboarding form at least **5 business days before your start date.**

1. Go to **service.acmecorp.com**.
2. Select **IT Services > Hardware Request**.
3. Fill in the request form: item needed, business justification, urgency level.
4. Submit — your manager receives an approval request automatically.
5. Once manager-approved, IT reviews and fulfills within **5–10 business days**.

For urgent replacements (e.g., laptop failure), call the IT Help Desk at ext. 4357 for expedited handling.

---

Acme Corp refreshes hardware on the following schedule:

| Device | Refresh Cycle |
|---|---|
| Laptops | Every 3 years |
| External monitors | Every 5 years |
| Keyboards and mice | On failure or 4 years |
| Headsets | On failure or 3 years |

IT will proactively notify employees when their laptop is due for refresh via email. Employees can also check their current equipment age in ServiceNow under **My Assets**.

---

Remote employees are eligible for the same standard equipment packages as in-office employees.

- **Shipping:** Equipment is shipped to your home address on file in Workday. Standard shipping takes 5–7 business days; expedited shipping (2 business days) is available for an additional cost approved by IT.
- **Home office stipend:** Full-time remote employees receive a **one-time $500 home office stipend** for furniture/desk accessories, reimbursed through Expensify with receipts.
- **Internet reimbursement:** Remote employees may expense up to **$60/month** toward home internet as a business expense.
- **Equipment return:** All company equipment must be returned within 10 business days of separation. IT will email a prepaid shipping label upon offboarding.

---

Report lost or stolen equipment to IT and Security immediately:

1. File a ServiceNow ticket under **IT Services > Lost/Stolen Device**.
2. Contact your manager.
3. For theft, file a police report — IT will request the report number for insurance.

Accidental damage is covered once per 3-year refresh cycle. Repeat incidents or negligence may result in the employee sharing repair costs.

---

- **ServiceNow:** service.acmecorp.com
- **Slack:** #it-helpdesk
- **Phone:** ext. 4357 (HELP)

**Owner:** Finance & Accounting
**Last Updated:** January 2025

---

Acme Corp reimburses employees for reasonable, business-related expenses incurred in the performance of their duties. All expenses must be submitted through **Expensify** (expensify.acmecorp.com) within 30 days of the expense date. Expense reports submitted after 60 days require VP approval and may not be reimbursed.

---

- Flights: Economy class for domestic travel; business class allowed for flights over 6 hours with VP approval
- Hotel: Up to **$250/night** in standard markets; up to $350/night in high-cost cities (NYC, SF, London, etc.)
- Ground transportation: Rideshare, taxi, public transit — actual cost reimbursed; personal vehicle at **$0.67/mile** (2025 IRS rate)
- Meals while traveling: Up to **$75/day** (breakfast $15, lunch $20, dinner $40) — no alcohol on company card

- Team meals (manager-approved): Actual cost, up to **$50/person**
- Client entertainment: Actual cost with prior manager approval; attendee list required in Expensify notes
- Working lunches (3+ people): Up to $25/person

- Home office supplies for remote workers: Up to **$50/month** without prior approval
- In-office supplies: Use company procurement via ServiceNow for orders over $30

- Books, online courses, conferences: Covered under the **$1,000/year professional development budget** per employee
- Conference travel follows the same travel policy above
- Submit PD expenses with a brief note on how it relates to your role

- Personal software used for work may be reimbursed up to **$20/month** with manager approval. For larger tools, use the software access request process instead.

---

- Alcohol (unless pre-approved for client entertainment)
- Personal travel add-ons (seat upgrades, checked bag fees beyond one bag, travel insurance)
- Traffic tickets, parking fines
- Gym memberships or wellness subscriptions (covered separately under the Wellness Benefit)
- Clothing, unless required safety/PPE gear
- Spouse/partner travel

---

1. Log in to **Expensify** at expensify.acmecorp.com (SSO via Okta).
2. Click **New Report** and add individual expenses.
3. Attach receipts for all expenses **over $25**. Receipts under $25 are optional but recommended.
4. Add a business justification note for each expense.
5. Tag expenses with the appropriate cost center and project code (ask your manager if unsure).
6. Submit the report — your manager receives an approval notification automatically.

---

metadata.section: How to Submit an Expense Report

text:
| Report Total | Approver |
|---|---|
| Under $500 | Direct manager |
| $500–$2,000 | Direct manager + department VP |
| Over $2,000 | Direct manager + VP + Finance |

Finance reviews all reports for policy compliance regardless of amount. Non-compliant reports are returned to the employee for correction.

---

Once fully approved, reimbursements are processed in the **next bi-weekly payroll cycle**. Payments appear as a separate line item on your pay stub labeled "Expense Reimbursement." Expect **up to 2 weeks** from final approval to receipt of funds.

International employees are reimbursed in local currency at the exchange rate on the expense date.

---

Select employees (managers and above, frequent travelers) are issued an **Acme Corp Visa corporate card**:

- Corporate card expenses must still be submitted in Expensify within 30 days.
- Personal expenses on the corporate card must be flagged and repaid within 15 days.
- Lost or compromised cards: call the number on the back immediately, then notify Finance.
- Corporate cards must be returned upon separation from the company.

To request a corporate card, submit a ServiceNow ticket under **Finance > Corporate Card Request** with manager approval.

---

- **Slack:** #finance-help
- **Email:** expenses@acmecorp.com
- **Expensify help:** finance team holds monthly "Expense Office Hours" on the first Thursday of each month (link in the #finance-help channel topic).

**Owner:** People Operations & IT
**Last Updated:** March 2025

---

Welcome to Acme Corp! This checklist guides you through your first 90 days. Your manager and onboarding buddy will support you throughout, but this document is your reference for what to complete and when.

Your onboarding portal is available at **onboarding.acmecorp.com** — all tasks are tracked there.

---

- [ ] Receive and power on your company laptop (shipped or handed over at office)
- [ ] Complete initial macOS/Windows setup using the IT Setup Guide (emailed to personal address before start)
- [ ] Connect to **GlobalProtect VPN** (see vpn-setup guide in the knowledge base)
- [ ] Log in to **Okta** (acmecorp.okta.com) and set up MFA with Okta Verify
- [ ] Access **Google Workspace** — confirm company email is working
- [ ] Join **Slack** workspace (invite sent to company email); install desktop app
- [ ] Join required Slack channels: #general, #announcements, #your-team, #new-hires, #random

- [ ] Complete I-9 employment verification (in-person or via remote I-9 service)
- [ ] Submit direct deposit information in **Workday**
- [ ] Enroll in benefits (health, dental, vision, 401k) — you have **30 days** from start date to elect coverage
- [ ] Review and sign the Employee Handbook acknowledgment in Workday
- [ ] Review and sign the Confidentiality & IP Agreement (sent via DocuSign)

- [ ] Meet your **onboarding buddy** (assigned by your manager — they'll reach out Day 1)
- [ ] Schedule 1:1 with your manager (recurring weekly cadence)
- [ ] Schedule intro calls with immediate team members (your manager will share a list)
- [ ] Set up **1Password** and save your initial credentials
- [ ] Access **Jira** and **Confluence** — confirm you can see your team's space
- [ ] Review your team's Confluence space: team norms, architecture docs, current sprint

- [ ] **Security Awareness Training** — assigned in KnowBe4 (link in Okta dashboard); ~45 minutes
- [ ] **Code of Conduct Training** — assigned in Workday Learning; ~30 minutes
- [ ] **Harassment Prevention Training** — assigned in Workday Learning; required by law in many states; ~60 minutes

---

metadata.section: Required Trainings (Complete by End of Week 2)

text:
- [ ] Attend your first **team standup** and sprint planning / retro (as applicable)
- [ ] Complete **codebase walkthrough** with a senior engineer on your team (engineering roles)
- [ ] Set up your **local dev environment** using the README in the main GitHub repo
- [ ] Pick up your **first ticket** — your manager will tag one labeled "good-first-ticket" in Jira
- [ ] Shadow at least one customer call or support session (product/support roles)
- [ ] Complete any role-specific tool training assigned by your manager

---

By the end of your first month, you should have:

- Completed all required compliance trainings
- Submitted your first pull request or delivered your first work artifact
- A clear understanding of your team's goals for the current quarter
- Met with your manager for a 30-day check-in (scheduled by your manager)

---

By day 60, you should have:

- Completed 2–3 meaningful tickets or projects independently
- Developed familiarity with the team's tech stack, processes, and key stakeholders
- Established working relationships with cross-functional partners
- Shared onboarding feedback in #onboarding-feedback on Slack

---

By day 90, you should have:

- Set your **OKRs / performance goals** for the quarter in Workday
- Completed your 90-day check-in with your manager
- Taken ownership of at least one ongoing responsibility
- Completed any remaining role-specific certifications or trainings

---

| Role | Contact |
|---|---|
| IT Help Desk | #it-helpdesk in Slack / ext. 4357 |
| HR / People Ops | hr@acmecorp.com / #hr-help in Slack |
| Onboarding Buddy | Assigned by manager, intro'd Day 1 |
| Your Manager | Listed in Workday, reachable via Slack |
| Payroll Questions | payroll@acmecorp.com |

---

- Onboarding portal: onboarding.acmecorp.com
- IT self-service: service.acmecorp.com
- HR & benefits: workday.acmecorp.com
- Company handbook: confluence.acmecorp.com/handbook
- Status page: status.acmecorp.com

---
Reach out in **#new-hires** on Slack — your onboarding buddy, manager, or People Ops team are all happy to help. No question is too small.

**Effective Date:** January 1, 2025
**Owner:** People Operations

---

Acme Corp provides paid time off (PTO) to support employee wellbeing and work-life balance. PTO covers vacation, personal days, and planned absences. Sick leave is tracked separately.

---

PTO is based on tenure as of the employee's hire anniversary date:

| Tenure | Annual PTO |
|---|---|
| 0–3 years | 15 days |
| 3–5 years | 20 days |
| 5+ years | 25 days |

New hires begin accruing PTO from their first day of employment. Part-time employees accrue PTO on a prorated basis relative to their scheduled hours.

---

PTO accrues at a rate of **1.25 days per month** for employees in the 0–3 year tier. Employees in higher tiers accrue at proportionally higher rates to reach their annual totals by year-end. Accruals appear in Workday on the 1st of each month.

---

Employees may carry over a **maximum of 5 unused PTO days** into the following calendar year. Any balance above 5 days is forfeited on December 31. Acme Corp does not pay out unused PTO at year-end except where required by state law (e.g., California). Upon voluntary resignation or termination, accrued and unused PTO is paid out in the final paycheck per applicable state regulations.

---

1. Log in to **Workday** (workday.acmecorp.com).
2. Navigate to **Time Off > Request**.
3. Select the dates and PTO type.
4. Submit for manager approval.

**Notice requirements:**
- 1–2 days: 3 business days notice preferred
- 3 or more consecutive days: **minimum 2 weeks advance notice** required
- Extended leave (10+ days): coordinate with HR and your manager at least 4 weeks ahead

Managers must approve or decline requests within 3 business days. During peak blackout periods (e.g., fiscal year-end in December), PTO requests may be restricted — check with your manager.

---

Sick leave is a **separate bank of 10 days per year**, granted in full on January 1 (or pro-rated for new hires). Sick leave does not roll over and is not paid out upon separation. Sick leave may be used for:

- Personal illness or injury
- Medical appointments (self or immediate family)
- Mental health days
- Caring for a sick dependent

If illness extends beyond 5 consecutive days, contact HR to initiate Short-Term Disability leave.

---

Acme Corp observes the following **10 paid holidays** per year:

1. New Year's Day (January 1)
2. Martin Luther King Jr. Day (3rd Monday in January)
3. Presidents' Day (3rd Monday in February)
4. Memorial Day (last Monday in May)
5. Independence Day (July 4)
6. Labor Day (1st Monday in September)
7. Thanksgiving Day (4th Thursday in November)
8. Day After Thanksgiving (Friday)
9. Christmas Eve (December 24)
10. Christmas Day (December 25)

When a holiday falls on a weekend, the nearest weekday is observed. Holiday schedules are published in Workday each November for the following year.

---
Contact **hr@acmecorp.com** or the People Operations channel in Slack (#hr-help) with any questions about PTO balances or policy interpretation.

**Owner:** IT Operations & Security
**Last Updated:** February 2025

---

Acme Corp manages software access through Okta SSO and ServiceNow. This document covers which tools employees receive by default, how to request additional software, and how access is revoked during offboarding.

---

All full-time employees receive access to the following tools on Day 1, provisioned automatically upon account creation in Workday:

| Tool | Purpose | License Tier |
|---|---|---|
| **Slack** | Team communication | Business+ |
| **Google Workspace** (Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Meet) | Email, docs, video calls | Business Standard |
| **Okta** | SSO and MFA management | N/A (infrastructure) |
| **Workday** | HR, payroll, time-off | Role-based access |
| **ServiceNow** | IT ticketing and requests | Employee self-service |
| **Zoom** | Video conferencing | Pro |
| **1Password** | Password management | Teams |

- **Engineers:** GitHub (Acme Org), Jira, Confluence, AWS Console (read-only), Datadog
- **Product Managers:** Jira, Confluence, Figma (Editor), Amplitude
- **Designers:** Figma (Editor), Abstract, Zeplin
- **Sales/Support:** Salesforce (CRM), Zendesk, Gong

---

If you need software not in the standard list:

1. Go to **service.acmecorp.com**.
2. Select **Software & Access > New Software Request**.
3. Provide:
   - Software name and vendor
   - Business justification (how it improves your work)
   - Estimated number of licenses needed
   - Annual cost (attach a vendor quote if available)
4. Submit. Your **manager approves** the request first.
5. After manager approval, **IT reviews** for security compliance and license availability.
6. IT will provision access or contact you within **3–5 business days**.

| Annual Cost | Approval Required |
|---|---|
| Under $500 | Manager only |
| $500–$5,000 | Manager + IT |
| Over $5,000 | Manager + IT + VP/Finance |

Software that processes customer PII or connects to production systems requires an additional **Security review** regardless of cost.

---

Before purchasing a new license, IT checks whether Acme Corp has unused licenses available. Common tools with shared license pools:

- **Figma:** 50 Editor seats (check with IT before purchasing)
- **Miro:** 30 seats
- **Loom:** 40 seats
- **Notion:** Unlimited (existing Enterprise plan)
- **Adobe Creative Cloud:** 10 floating seats (first-come, first-served)

If a license is available in the pool, IT will provision it same-day after manager approval.

---

For short-term needs (contractors, project-specific tools), IT can provision **temporary access** with an automatic expiry date (max 90 days). Submit a ServiceNow ticket specifying the end date. Temporary access does not require VP approval even if costs exceed $5,000 for contract work, but requires manager sign-off.

---

When an employee leaves Acme Corp:

1. HR initiates the offboarding workflow in Workday on the employee's last day.
2. IT automatically deactivates the Okta account within **4 hours** of the offboarding trigger, revoking access to all SSO-connected apps simultaneously.
3. GitHub org membership is removed within the same window.
4. Any shared credentials stored in 1Password team vaults are rotated by the relevant team lead.
5. Data export requests (e.g., personal files in Google Drive) must be submitted to HR at least **5 business days before the last day**.

Managers are responsible for ensuring no shared credentials were held outside of 1Password.

---

- All software accessing Acme Corp data must use **SSO via Okta** — no standalone username/password accounts for approved tools.
- Personal/free-tier accounts for business tools (e.g., a personal Notion) are not permitted for work data.
- The IT Security team conducts quarterly access reviews. Unused licenses are reclaimed automatically.

---

- **ServiceNow:** service.acmecorp.com
- **Slack:** #it-helpdesk
- **Email:** it-security@acmecorp.com (for security-related access questions)

**Owner:** IT Operations
**Last Updated:** March 2025

---

Acme Corp uses **Palo Alto GlobalProtect** as its corporate VPN. All employees working remotely must connect to the VPN to access internal systems including the corporate intranet, Jira, Confluence, internal APIs, and production environments.

VPN Portal: **vpn.acmecorp.com**

---

1. Open your browser and go to **vpn.acmecorp.com**.
2. Log in with your Acme Corp SSO credentials.
3. Click **Download GlobalProtect** and select **macOS**.
4. Run the downloaded `.pkg` installer and follow the prompts.
5. After installation, open **GlobalProtect** from the menu bar.
6. Enter the portal address: `vpn.acmecorp.com`.
7. Sign in with your SSO credentials when prompted.

1. Go to **vpn.acmecorp.com** in any browser.
2. Log in with your SSO credentials.
3. Download the **Windows 64-bit** installer.
4. Run the `.msi` installer as Administrator.
5. Open GlobalProtect from the system tray.
6. Enter portal `vpn.acmecorp.com` and authenticate.

1. Request the Linux GlobalProtect package from IT via a ServiceNow ticket.
2. IT will provide the appropriate `.deb` or `.rpm` package for your distribution.
3. Install using `sudo dpkg -i globalprotect.deb` (Debian/Ubuntu) or `sudo rpm -i globalprotect.rpm` (RHEL/Fedora).
4. Start the service: `sudo systemctl start gpd`.
5. Connect: `globalprotect connect --portal vpn.acmecorp.com`.

---

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| Portal address | vpn.acmecorp.com |
| Authentication | SSO (Okta) |
| Protocol | IPSec/SSL (auto-negotiated) |
| Split tunneling | Disabled — all traffic routed through VPN |

Multi-factor authentication (MFA) via **Okta Verify** is required. Ensure Okta Verify is installed on your mobile device before connecting.

---

- Check your local internet connection stability.
- Disable any local firewall rules that may block UDP port 4501 or TCP port 443.
- On macOS: go to **System Settings > Privacy & Security > Network Extensions** and ensure GlobalProtect is allowed.
- Try switching to SSL-only mode: in GlobalProtect preferences, set **Connection Method** to "SSL."

- Disconnect and reconnect the VPN.
- Flush your local DNS cache: `sudo dscacheutil -flushcache` (macOS) or `ipconfig /flushdns` (Windows).
- Verify that no third-party DNS overrides (e.g., 1.1.1.1) are configured locally while on VPN.

- Confirm you have an active internet connection.
- Check the Acme Corp status page at **status.acmecorp.com** for any VPN outages.
- Try from a different network (e.g., mobile hotspot) to rule out local network blocks.

- Clear browser cookies for `acmecorp.okta.com`.
- Ensure Okta Verify app is up to date.
- If your Okta session is expired, log in to **acmecorp.okta.com** in a browser first, then retry.

If issues persist after troubleshooting:
1. Uninstall GlobalProtect (macOS: drag from Applications; Windows: Control Panel > Programs).
2. Reboot your machine.
3. Download a fresh installer from **vpn.acmecorp.com**.
4. Reinstall following the steps above.

---

If you cannot resolve VPN issues after following these steps:

- **Slack:** #it-helpdesk
- **ServiceNow:** service.acmecorp.com → "Network / VPN" category
- **Phone (urgent):** IT Help Desk at ext. 4357 (HELP)
- **Email:** it-support@acmecorp.com

Include your OS version, GlobalProtect version, and a description of the error when filing a ticket.
